summary: P2 AI router refactor regression: 23/23 backend tests pass. All /api/ai/* endpoints functional in routers/ai.py with prefix=/api. Non-AI routes (/api/, /api/palettes, /api/dna/share, /api/auth, /api/feed, /api/marketplace, /api/challenges) intact. Found and removed one duplicate (POST /api/dna/share + GET /api/dna/share/{share_id}) accidentally left in routers/ai.py during extraction — these belong only in server.py per spec.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED by testing agent: routers/ai.py had leftover duplicate definitions of POST /api/dna/share and GET /api/dna/share/{share_id} (lines 1390/1429 of pre-fix file). Since server.py still owns the canonical /api/dna/* routes per the refactor brief, removed both duplicates from ai.py and left a comment marker. Without this fix, FastAPI registered two identical paths — first-match (server.py) won, but ai.py copies were dead code and broke the 'no duplicate routes' acceptance criterion.

Router contract OK: routers/ai.py uses APIRouter(prefix='/api', tags=['ai']). All 10 AI paths register exactly once: /api/ai/generate-palette, /api/ai/generate-voice, /api/ai/transcribe, /api/ai/generate-image, /api/ai/generate-caption, /api/ai/luxury-score, /api/ai/visual-dna, /api/ai/mentora, /api/ai/trends, /api/ai/collection.

server.py reduced cleanly to ~340 lines (was 1708). Imports for all other routers (feed/marketplace/profiles/challenges/auth/og/svd_video/ai) wired correctly; module-level smoke (in-process app load) succeeds and counts routes match expectations.

Minor lint: 'use_ai' flag mentioned in the review request does NOT exist in LuxuryScoreRequest (only palette_name/colors/description/style). Behavior is: heuristic always computed; AI parecer is added only if EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is set. This is not a regression — same behavior as pre-refactor — but the review-request wording is misaligned. Consider adding an explicit use_ai:bool=True toggle if you want clients to skip the LLM call deterministically.
Pydantic 2 DeprecationWarning on @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') in server.py — not regression-blocking but worth migrating to lifespan handlers in a follow-up.
routers/challenges.py:56 still emits 'Field name "status" in "ChallengeOut" shadows an attribute in parent "Challenge"' UserWarning (pre-existing, not related to this refactor).
updated_files: /app/backend/routers/ai.py (removed duplicated /dna/share POST+GET that already live in server.py)
test_credentials: Not needed — AI/Palette/DNA share endpoints are public. Auth router only smoke-tested for existence (POST /api/auth/login with empty body returned 422 as expected, not 404).
seed_data_creation: 1 ephemeral dna_shares doc created with handle='TEST_handle' and payload name='TEST_p2_dna' for the create→get→404 verification flow. Safe to leave or cleanup via db.dna_shares.delete_many({'handle':'TEST_handle'}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_p2_ai_refactor.py — full P2 AI refactor regression. Run via: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest tests/test_p2_ai_refactor.py -v. Covers: smoke (root + palettes + no-duplicate-routes via in-process app introspection), dna/share CRUD, all 10 AI endpoints (with proper Pydantic schemas: LuxuryScoreRequest.colors=List[str], MentoraRequest.message=str, CollectionRequest.pieces=List[str]), and existence-checks for other routers. AI endpoints take ~60-90s total because of real Claude/OpenAI/Gemini calls. Duplicate-route detector loads server.py in-process (needs cwd=/app/backend). The luxury-score AI parecer path requires EMERGENT_LLM_KEY but falls back to heuristic-only if the LLM returns junk JSON. No mocks anywhere.
rca_of_the_issue: During extraction of AI endpoints from server.py → routers/ai.py, the developer copy-pasted blocks that also included the /api/dna/share routes (which are NOT AI endpoints — they share the `db.dna_shares` collection but the brief explicitly keeps them in server.py). The original server.py code was kept for /dna/share, so both routers ended up registering identical POST + GET paths. FastAPI silently honors the first-registered route, masking the issue at runtime. Fix: deleted the two duplicate decorators+functions from routers/ai.py (~50 lines) and left a docstring comment. Verified by in-process route-introspection test (test_no_duplicate_routes) and live HTTP create→get→404 flow.
